Notice Of Motion And Motion For Evidence Sanctions Against Defendant Haystack Pizaa, Inc., Filed On Behalf Of Plaintff Jose Iriarte
Off Calendar Dec-08-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., To Respond To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., In The Amount Of $2,850.00 Filed On Behalf Of Plaintiff, Jose Iriarte
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances in Open Court. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

3 - PLAINTIFF JOSE IRIARTE's Motion To Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., To Respond To Form Interrogatories-Employment Law, For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., In The Amount Of $2,850.00 Filed On Behalf Of Plaintiff, Jose Iriarte.

Plaintiff Jose Iriarte's Motion To Compel Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc.'s Responses To Form Interrogatories-Employment and for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED.

The discovery request at issue appears reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Defendant has not established that he has provided any responses to the interrogatories. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for Form Interrogatories-Employment when no responses have been provided. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290.)

Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. shall serve a verified, objection-free, substantive response to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories-Employment by October 15, 2025.

Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. did not act with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. shall pay $1,060 to Jose Iriarte as sanctions, payment no later than October 24, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure 2030.290(c).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., To Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,900.00 Against Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., Filed On Behalf Of Plaintiff, Jose Iriarte
Off Calendar Sep-24-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances in open court The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - PLAINTIFF JOSE IRIARTE's Motion To Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., To Respond To Request For Production Of Documents, For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $1,900.00 Against Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., Filed On Behalf Of Plaintiff, Jose Iriarte

Plaintiff Jose Iriarte's Motion To Compel Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc.'s Response To Plaintiff's Demand For Production Of Documents and for Sanctions is GRANTED.

The discovery requests at issue appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) The record demonstrates Defendant did not provide any responses to the RFPs. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for requests for production of documents when no responses have been provided. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.300.)

Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. shall serve verified, objection-free, responses to Plaintiff's RFPs, Set One by October 15, 2025.

Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. did not act with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. shall pay $1,060 to Jose Iriarte as sanctions, payment no later than October 24, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure 2031.300(c).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza,,Inc., To Respond To Special Interrogatori Es, For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant, Haystack Pizza,, Inc9 In The Amount Of $2,850.00 Filed On Behalf Of Plaintiff, Jose Iriarte
Off Calendar Sep-17-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JOSE IRIARTE INDIVIDUALLY, AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER AGGRIEVED EMPLOYEES Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza,,Inc., To Respond To Special Interrogatori Es, For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant, Haystack Pizza,, Inc9 In The Amount Of $2,850.00 Filed On Behalf Of Plaintiff, Jose Iriarte

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOSE IRIARTE's Motion To Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza,,Inc., To Respond To Special Interrogatori Es, For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant, Haystack Pizza,, Inc9 In The Amount Of $2,850.00 Filed On Behalf Of Plaintiff, Jose Iriarte.

Plaintiff Jose Iriarte's unopposed Motion to Compel Defendant, Haystack Pizza, Inc., to Respond to Special Interrogatories [sic], for Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED.

The discovery requests at issue-form interrogatories-employment-appear reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2017.010.) Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. has not established that it has provided any responses to the form interrogatories. No meet and confer requirement exists for motions to compel responses for Form Interrogatories when no responses have been provided. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290.)

Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. shall serve verified, objection-free, substantive responses to Plaintiff Jose Iriarte's Form Interrogatories-Employment, Set One by October 2, 2025.

Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. did not act with substantial justification in connection with this discovery. Defendant Haystack Pizza, Inc. shall pay $1160 to Plaintiff Jose Iriarte. as sanctions, payment no later than October 9, 2025. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.010, 2023.030 and 2030.290.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
